title_en: Ibn Khallikan: Jurist, Judge, and Master Biographer
title_ar: ابن خلكان: الفقيه والقاضي والمؤرخ البارع
content_en:
Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khallikan (608–681 AH / 1211–1282 CE) was a distinguished Shafi'i jurist and judge whose most enduring legacy is his monumental biographical dictionary Wafayat al-A'yan wa-Anba Abna az-Zaman — the Deaths of Eminent Men and the News of the Sons of the Age. Born in Irbil (Erbil, in present-day Iraq) into a scholarly family with roots in the ancient city of Balkh, he received a classical Islamic education in Irbil and traveled widely for further study in Mosul, Aleppo, Cairo, and Damascus.

Ibn Khallikan spent significant periods of his scholarly career in Egypt, where he taught and served in judicial capacities, before being appointed chief judge (qadi al-qudat) of Syria based in Damascus, a position he held on two separate occasions with an intervening period of removal and return. His judicial career brought him into contact with the leading political, intellectual, and cultural figures of the Ayyubid and early Mamluk periods, giving him access to information and networks that enriched his biographical work.

His formation was intellectual as well as legal: he was well versed in Arabic literature, poetry, and the histories of previous Islamic scholars, and his writing style reflects the refined literary culture of the medieval Islamic scholarly class. Unlike some biographical scholars who wrote in a spare technical style, Ibn Khallikan wrote with literary elegance, incorporating poetry and anecdote to illuminate his subjects' characters.

The Wafayat represents decades of scholarly labor. Ibn Khallikan began working on it during his first period in Egypt and continued revising it throughout his later career. The work draws on an extraordinary range of sources — biographical dictionaries, historical chronicles, literary anthologies, and personal inquiry — and reflects Ibn Khallikan's determination to produce a work of both scholarly reliability and literary quality.

His death in Damascus in 681 AH came after a career of tremendous scholarly productivity. The Wafayat stands alone as his great legacy, but contemporaries also valued him as a teacher and legal authority. His students included scholars who carried on the biographical tradition he had elevated, and his influence on the genre of Islamic biography is difficult to overstate.
title_id: Ibn Khallikan: Ahli Hukum, Hakim, dan Ahli Biografi Unggul
content_id:
Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khallikan (608–681 H / 1211–1282 M) adalah seorang ahli hukum Syafi'i dan hakim yang terkemuka, yang warisan terbesarnya adalah kamus biografi monumentalnya Wafayat al-A'yan wa-Anba Abna az-Zaman — Wafat Para Tokoh Terkemuka dan Berita tentang Putra-Putra Zaman. Lahir di Irbil (Erbil, di Iraq sekarang) dalam keluarga ilmiah yang berakar di kota kuno Balkh, beliau mendapatkan pendidikan Islam klasik di Irbil dan mengembara secara luas untuk studi lebih lanjut di Mosul, Aleppo, Kairo, dan Damaskus.

Ibn Khallikan menghabiskan periode penting karier keilmuannya di Mesir, di mana beliau mengajar dan bertugas dalam kapasitas yudisial, sebelum diangkat sebagai hakim agung (qadi al-qudat) Suriah berkedudukan di Damaskus, jabatan yang beliau emban pada dua kesempatan terpisah dengan periode pemecatan dan pengembalian di antaranya. Karier yudisialnya membawa beliau berkontak dengan tokoh-tokoh politik, intelektual, dan budaya terkemuka pada masa Ayyubid dan Mamluk awal, memberinya akses ke informasi dan jaringan yang memperkaya karya biografinya.

Pembentukannya bersifat intelektual maupun hukum: beliau sangat menguasai sastra Arab, puisi, dan sejarah para ulama Islam terdahulu, dan gaya penulisannya mencerminkan budaya sastra yang halus dari kelas ilmiah Islam abad pertengahan. Tidak seperti sebagian ulama biografi yang menulis dengan gaya teknis yang lugas, Ibn Khallikan menulis dengan keanggunan sastra, memasukkan puisi dan anekdot untuk menerangi karakter para tokoh yang dibiografikan.

Wafayat mewakili kerja keilmuan selama beberapa dekade. Ibn Khallikan mulai mengerjakannya selama periode pertamanya di Mesir dan terus merevisinya sepanjang karier akhirnya, memanfaatkan kamus biografi, kronik sejarah, dan antologi sastra dalam jumlah yang luar biasa.
